items.correct: Three sections: A, contrasting B, and a return to A (ABA).
items.why: Students assume 'three sections' means three different ones. Ternary has only TWO distinct ideas, but A returns - it's the return that makes it ternary, not the section count alone.

items.q: How many crotchets are in a semibreve?
items.why: Students confuse the relative durations. A semibreve (whole note) = 2 minims = 4 crotchets = 8 quavers. Each level halves in length.

items.q: Which orchestral family does the saxophone belong to?
items.misconception: Brass (because it is made of brass).
items.why: Students classify by material. Instrument families are defined by SOUND PRODUCTION: woodwinds use a reed or air across an edge. The sax uses a reed, so despite the brass body, it's woodwind.

items.q: A dot after a note adds?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: A half of its value. “A quarter of its value” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many quavers equal a crotchet?
items.why: The real figure is 2 quavers. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
